title: n8n vs Zapier – Which is right for you?
text: If you've encountered limitations with Zapier's flexibility, feature gating, or the unpredictable scaling costs of task-based workflows, you might find n8n appealing. With n8n, you can build workflows using custom Javascript or Python code at any step for flexibility and granular control. With n8n's LangChain nodes, you can easily incorporate AI-powered features into your workflows. Simply speaking, n8n and Zapier are addressing similar needs – data integration and workflow automation – but are made for use cases with different levels of complexity and customization. If you’re ready to move beyond the limitations of task-based tools and build the powerful, cost-effective automations your business needs, n8n’s team can help migrate your existing workflows to a more flexible and scalable platform.

title: How to trigger webhooks from Notion databases and buttons
text: You can trigger API automations from Notion databases or pages by sending a request to a webhook URL in a third-party service. ## How to add the webhook URL to Notion (and pass URL parameters) Once you have the webhook URL, you can add it to Notion on either a page or a database property. If you add a webhook URL to a Notion page, you can only pass *static* parameters (i.e., their value always remains the same). If you add a webhook URL to a Notion database property, you can pass *dynamic* parameters that reference the value of other properties. To add the webhook URL to a Notion database:
